thoughts:

I'm curious about how these different fields can be connected or how they might influence each other. The variety of links suggests that there's no one fixed definition of art, which is fascinating.

The difference in the nature of parallel lines in hyperbolic and elliptical geometries is also captivating. I'd like to explore this idea further.

It makes me wonder how knowledge was spread and preserved during that time. I'm also intrigued by the fact that the work is considered one of the most published books in history, with over 1000 editions. I'm also curious about the fact that Dresde was once a UNESCO World Heritage Site, but lost its status due to the construction of a bridge near the historic center. article: Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando
thoughts: I'm fascinated by the early history of the Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando. The fact that it was created as a response to the need for a new palace after the Alcázar fire in 1734 is intriguing. I'm also curious about the role of Juan Domingo Olivieri, the escultor de la Casa Real, in its formation. The fact that he maintained an academy of sculpture in his own rooms from 1741 and later became the director general of the new institution is impressive. I'm also interested in the shif...

I'm intrigued by the life and work of Juan Domingo Olivieri. His journey from being an Italian sculptor to becoming a prominent figure in Madrid's artistic scene is fascinating. I'm drawn to the details about his involvement in the creation of the Real Academia de Bellas Artes de San Fernando and the Palacio Nuevo's decoration. The fact that he promoted the formation of an academy of sculpture and later became its first director general is impressive. The description of the ornamental project fo...
